accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context (Section 547 PHSA, 42 U.S.C. 290ee-2, BCOR grant program).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the program description and statute references are accurate context confirmed by external sources.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill reauthorizes the building communities of recovery grant program by revising its funding authorization.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies the amendment to Section 547 of the Public Health Service Act and the strike-and-replace of funding language.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions or conditions; the summary correctly notes none exist.

effective_dates.why: The summary accurately notes the fiscal year span (2026-2030) and that there is no explicit effective date beyond enactment.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the sole operative effect—the funding reauthorization amount and applicable fiscal years.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and free of advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized, internally consistent, and reads clearly.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately brief with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the Public Health Service Act and the building communities of recovery program.

correct_figures.why: All figures—$5,000,000 (FY2019-2023) and $17,000,000 (FY2026-2030)—match the bill text exactly.
